mission.title: argo: Support for DeleteNamespace SyncPolicy
mission.description: Support for DeleteNamespace SyncPolicy. This issue affects 95+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app=argo
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n argo --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Support for DeleteNamespace SyncPolicy

steps.title: Check current Namespace configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo resources:
```bash
kubectl get namespace -A
kubectl describe namespace <name> -n argo
```
Adding support for some mechanism to allow ArgoCD to delete the namespace of its underlying app. This could be limited to apps that were created with a `CreateNamespace` SyncPolicy set.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for DeleteNamespace SyncPolicy
steps.description:
- After this PR is merged, we can specify `DeleteNamespace` syncOption, which enables a feature to delete an auto-created namespace.
- We can delete a namespace only when:
  - `CreateNamespace=true` and `DeleteNamespace=true`
  - The target namespace is tracked by an application. 
    - e.g.) The
```yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: guestbook
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc/
    namespace: test
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/argoproj/argocd-example-apps.git
    path: guestbook
    targetRevision: HEAD
  syncPolicy:
    syncOptions:
    - CreateNamespace=true
    - DeleteNamespace=true
    managedNamespaceMetadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: guestbook
```

steps.title: Confirm Support for DeleteNamespace SyncPolicy is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n argo --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: - After this PR is merged, we can specify `DeleteNamespace` syncOption, which enables a feature to delete an auto-created namespace.
- We can delete a namespace only when:
  - `CreateNamespace=true` and `DeleteNamespace=true`
  - The target namespace is tracked by an application. 
    - e.g.) The namespace must have a label, `app.kubernetes.io/instance=${applicationName}`.

apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: guestbook
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc/
    namespace: test
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/argoproj/argocd-example-apps.git
    path: guestbook
    targetRevision: HEAD
  syncPolicy:
    syncOptions:
    - CreateNamespace=true
    - DeleteNamespace=true
    managedNamespaceMetadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: guestbook

apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: guestbook
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: test
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/argoproj/argocd-example-apps.git
    path: guestbook
    targetRevision: HEAD
  syncPolicy:
    syncOptions:
    - CreateNamespace=true
    - DeleteNamespace=true
    managedNamespaceMetadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: guestbook

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
